messages.content: flutter add vertical separator

To add a vertical separator in Flutter, you can use the `VerticalDivider` widget. 

Example usage:

```dart
Column(
  children: [
    Text('Column item 1'),
    VerticalDivider(),
    Text('Column item 2'),
  ],
)
```

This will create a column with a vertical divider between the two text widgets.

You can customize the divider color, thickness, and indent by passing in parameters like so:

```dart
VerticalDivider(
  color: Colors.red,
  thickness: 2,
  indent: 10,
  endIndent: 10,
)
```

The `indent` parameter specifies the space before the divider, and `endIndent` specifies the space after the divider. If you only want the divider to appear on one side, use the `direction` parameter with the `VerticalDividerDirection.left` or `VerticalDividerDirection.right` options.
flutter int floor import

The `int` and `floor` functions are part of the Dart language itself and don't require any specific import. They are available by default in any Flutter or Dart project.

Here's an example of using `int` and `floor` functions to floor a double value and convert it to an integer:

```dart
double numDouble = 3.14;
int numInt = numDouble.floor(); // This will return 3 as an integer
```

Note that the `floor` method returns a `double` which needs to be explicitly cast to an `int` using the `int.parse` method:

```dart
double numDouble = 3.14;
int numInt = int.parse(numDouble.floor().toString()); // This will return 3 as an integer
```

flutter add BoxDecoration in a row

To add a `BoxDecoration` to a `Row` in Flutter, you can use the `Container` widget wrapped around your `Row` and set the `decoration` property of the `Container` to your desired `BoxDecoration`. Here's an example:

```dart
Container(
  decoration: BoxDecoration(
    borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(10),
    color: Colors.grey[300],
  ),
  child: Row(
    children: [
      Icon(Icons.account_box),
      SizedBox(width: 10),
      Text('Username'),
    ],
  ),
);
```

In this example, a `Container` widget with a `BoxDecoration` is used to wrap around a `Row` that contains an icon and a text widget. The `BoxDecoration` has a `borderRadius` of `10` and a `color` of `Colors.grey[300]`, which creates a rounded grey background behind the entire row. 

You can customize the `BoxDecoration` properties according to your needs. Keep in mind that `BoxDecoration` can accept more properties like `border`, `gradient`, and `boxShadow`.
flutter Container decoration padding from child

To add padding between the contents of a `Container` and its `BoxDecoration` in Flutter, you can use the `padding` property of the `Container`. Here's an example:

```dart
Container(
  decoration: BoxDecoration(
    borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(10),
    color: Colors.grey[300],
  ),
  padding: EdgeInsets.all(10), // add padding to the entire container
  child: Row(
    children: [
      Icon(Icons.account_box),
      SizedBox(width: 10),
      Text('Username'),
    ],
  ),
);
```

In this example, the `padding` property is set to `EdgeInsets.all(10)`, which adds padding of 10 pixels around the entire `Container`, including its contents. 

If you want to add padding between the contents of the `Container` and its `BoxDecoration` only, you can wrap the `Row` widget inside a `Padding` widget and provide the desired padding as needed. Here's an example:

```dart
Container(
  decoration: BoxDecoration(
    borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(10),
    color: Colors.grey[300],
  ),
  child: Padding(
    padding: EdgeInsets.all(10), // add padding between the contents and the decoration
    child: Row(
      children: [
        Icon(Icons.account_box),
        SizedBox(width: 10),
        Text('Username'),
      ],
    ),
  ),
);
```

In this example, the `Padding` widget is used to wrap around the `Row` widget and provide the desired padding between the contents and the `BoxDecoration`. 

You can customize the amount of padding according to your needs by adjusting the `EdgeInsets` values.
flutter Container  BoxDecoration height adjust

You can adjust the height of a `Container` with a `BoxDecoration` in Flutter by setting the `height` property of the `Container`. Here's an example:

```dart
Container(
  height: 200,
  decoration: BoxDecoration(
    borderRadius: BorderRadius.circular(10),
    color: Colors.grey[300],
  ),
  child: Row(
    children: [
      Icon(Icons.account_box),
      SizedBox(width: 10),
      Text('Username'),
    ],
  ),
);
```

In this example, the `height` property of the `Container` is set to `200`, which gives the container a fixed height of 200 pixels. The `BoxDecoration` has a `borderRadius` of `10` and a `color` of `Colors.grey[300]`, which creates a rounded grey background behind the entire row.

You can adjust the `height` property to any value you need. Keep in mind that setting a fixed height can affect the responsiveness of your UI, especially on different screen sizes or orientations. In some cases, it may be better to use flexible widgets like `Expanded` or `Flexible` inside a `Column` or `Row` to allow the widgets to dynamically adjust their size based on the available space.
add bottom line for appbar

To add a bottom line to the `AppBar` in Flutter, you can use the `bottom` property and set it to a `PreferredSize` widget with a `preferredSize` and `child` property. Here's an example:

```dart
AppBar(
  title: Text('My App'),
  bottom: PreferredSize(
    preferredSize: Size.fromHeight(1),
    child: Container(
      color: Colors.grey,
    ),
  ),
);
```

In this example, the `bottom` property of the `AppBar` is set to a `PreferredSize` widget with a `preferredSize` of `Size.fromHeight(1)` and a child `Container` with a `color` of `Colors.grey`. This will add a 1-pixel grey line at the bottom of the `AppBar`.

You can adjust the height and color of the line by changing the `preferredSize` height and the `color` property of the `Container`, respectively.

Keep in mind that using a `PreferredSize` widget with a custom bottom widget can affect the default behavior of the `AppBar`, such as how it scrolls with the `NestedScrollView`. If you need to use a more complex bottom widget with scroll behavior, consider using a `SliverAppBar` instead.
